summary:
Both tRNA and U6 snRNA are processed differently; they don't need 5' capping as part of their functional roles within cells. In contrast, mRNA benefits significantly from being capped at the 5' end to ensure proper stabilization and translational efficiency. \boxed{C}

The summary accurately captures that both tRNA and U6 snRNA lack the requirement for 5' capping based on specific biological reasons related to their distinct functionalities among these options provided by focusing only on clinically significant aspects directly addressing why each type behaves uniquely regarding post-transcriptional modifications necessary or unnecessary during gene expression processes.
